title: COMPUTATIONAL MODEL FOR STRUCTURE DETERMINATION OF THE Na4Ag44(P-MBA)30 NANOPARTICLE SUPERSTRUCTURE
author: Guadron-Escobar, Victor
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: The purpose of this study is to determine the viability of the use of the Rietveld method to solve the structure of the Na4Ag44(p-MBA)30 material. Na4Ag44(p-MBA)30 is a chemical solid in the form of a crystal that has potential use in sensors, acceleration of chemical reactions, and medicine. It has been previously produced by researchers at the University of Toledo who used the Le Bail method to investigate its structure at different pressures. However, the Le Bail method does not provide information about the arrangement of atoms in a material. The Rietveld refinement is a more rigorous method of investigating this characteristic of solids. Additionally, the simulated annealing and rigid bodies techniques simplify the Rietveld method. However, the experimental data must contain enough information to obtain an accurate structure. In this investigation the MATLAB program was used to determine if the data available can be used to perform a Rietveld refinement. The analysis with this program showed that the data on the Na4Ag44(p-MBA)30 material at different pressures cannot be used in the Rietveld method, as any analysis could result in an inaccurate representation of the crystal structure.
